Women in AI Event · Booth Playbook

Show up savvy.
Stand out smart.

Everything Wendy and Bridgette need to run a brilliant, on-brand booth — without blowing the budget.

Full booth concept

01 — Visual presence

Display & signage

Pull-up banner — robot mascot, tagline & URL

🪧

Pull-up banner

  • Deep purple #5B5BD6 background with white Savvy logo top
  • Robot mascot large and centred — friendly and eye-catching
  • Tagline — "We talk to robots so you don't have to."
  • savvyautomate.com in teal at the base
  • Portrait format, positioned to the left behind the table
🖥️

Backdrop wall banner

  • Large fabric banner behind the table — "We talk to robots so you don't have to."
  • Circuit board graphic details in purple on purple
  • Acts as a photo backdrop — great for social content on the day
Tip: The pull-up banner should be visible from across the room. Go big with the robot — it's your crowd magnet.

02 — Table detail

Table setup close-up

Purple table runner, laptop demo, branded merch & "What's eating your time?" jar

🫙

"What's eating your time?" jar

  • People write their biggest manual task on a card and drop it in
  • Best conversation starter on the table — feels personal, not salesy
  • Post the best ones to LinkedIn after the event
💻

Laptop demo

  • Branded slide looping — "Streamline & Automate"
  • Shows a real n8n or Zapier workflow running live
📋

QR tent card

  • "Scan for something free!" — links to a free resource or booking form
  • Highest ROI item on the table for lead capture

03 — Team look

What Wendy & Bridgette wear

Matching black tees — Savvy logo front, "I automate the boring stuff. You're welcome." on the back

👕

Branded tees

  • Black crew neck — white Savvy logo left chest
  • Back of tee — "I automate the boring stuff. You're welcome."
  • Comfortable, intentional, memorable
  • Already visible in the background of the full booth shot!
Back reads: "I automate the boring stuff. You're welcome."
🪪

Accessories

  • Lanyards — purple with white logo, holds name badge
  • Optional cap — black with embroidered purple logo
  • Optional tote — useful carry bag for the day

04 — Things people actually keep

Low-cost giveaways

🤖

Robot mascot stickers

  • Die-cut sticker of the robot mascot — 50–100 units
  • Cheapest item at any event, most likely to be kept
  • Goes on laptops, notebooks, water bottles
  • Walking Savvy advertising long after the event
✏️

Branded stationery

  • Purple pens — "Savvy Automate — stop doing it manually"
  • Purple notepads — white lightbulb logo on cover
  • Order 30 of each — enough without overspending
☕

Branded mugs

  • White ceramic mugs with purple Savvy logo
  • Visible on the table — doubles as display and giveaway
  • Higher perceived value without huge cost
📱

Free Automation Audit card

  • A6 printed card — "Book your free 30-min Automation Audit"
  • QR code to Calendly or Tally booking form
  • Positions you as experts, not sellers

05 — Drawing people in

Engagement & lead capture

🫙

"What's eating your time?" jar

  • People write their biggest manual task on a card and drop it in
  • Creates genuine conversation — not a sales pitch
  • Post the best answers to LinkedIn — free content after the event
⚡

Live 2-min demo

  • Show one real n8n or Zapier workflow running live on the laptop
  • Pick something visual — email automation, Slack notification
  • Nothing converts better than watching it happen in real time
📊

QR lead capture

  • Tally form — name, email, biggest automation pain point
  • Offer a free resource on submit — checklist or workflow template
  • Collects leads without awkward "can I have your card?" moments

06 — Copy ideas

Tagline options

"We talk to robots so you don't have to." "Your business, on autopilot." "Less admin. More everything else." "Stop doing it manually." "The no-code automation people."
Tent card header: "Drowning in manual tasks? We build automations that fix that."

07 — Money

Rough budget breakdown

Pull-up banner
R800–1,400
2× branded tees
R600–900
Table runner
R700–1,200
Stickers (50× die-cut)
R300–500
Pens + notepads (×30)
R800–1,500
Mugs (×10)
R400–700
Tent cards + print
R200–400
Lanyards (×2)
R150–300
Total estimate
R3,950–6,900
Still well under budget at the high end — leaves room for caps, a tote bag giveaway, or a jar of purple sweets on the table.

Don't underestimate the mascot

That little robot is pure gold. Use it big on the banner, die-cut it as a sticker, and print it on the tee back. At an AI event, a friendly robot mascot is a genuine crowd-puller — it signals approachable expertise, not intimidating tech.
